question.content: Determine the products labeled A, B, C, D, and E for the three transformations (intramolecular lactonization, isotopic hydrolysis, and maltose hydrolysis/mutarotation). Output exactly one token sequence in the format A(...)*B(...)*C(...)*D(...)*E(...), plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Identify the three independent transformations: intramolecular lactonization, isotopic hydrolysis, and maltose hydrolysis mutarotation set.
step_1.key_point: Map each subquestion to one canonical product set.

step_3.content: For (A), acid-catalyzed intramolecular esterification of the hydroxy acid gives the ethyl-substituted delta-lactone major product.
step_3.key_point: Ring closure product is the dominant lactone.

step_5.content: For (B), opening the lactone in excess H2O18 under acid gives 4-hydroxybutanoic acid with both carboxyl oxygens labeled as 18O after exchange.
step_5.key_point: Both oxygen atoms in the carboxyl group are 18O.

step_7.content: For (C), maltose hydrolysis yields D-glucose forms represented by alpha anomer, open-chain form, and beta anomer.
step_7.key_point: Product set is alpha-D-glucose, open-chain D-glucose, beta-D-glucose.
